name: Ansar al-Sahel
composite_of: Composite closely modeled on JNIM and its precursors (Ansar Dine, AQIM's Saharan katibas, MUJAO, Katiba Macina). Locally rooted, federated structure, shadow-governance playbook.
notes: The entrenchment-first antagonist. Relations with the Azawad front are deliberately ambivalent (-0.1): allied in 2012, at war by 2013, coordinating again by 2026 — the affinity floor (§18.5) keeps the late alliance reachable.

name: Wilayat Sahel
composite_of: Composite closely modeled on IS-Sahel (ex-ISGS) and ISWAP's Lake Chad presence. Transnational franchise logic, higher-tempo violence, weaker local rooting.
notes: The strength-first antagonist. Starts only at the Lake Chad rim (the ISWAP face of the composite); the Saharan franchise arrives mid-run via the 'franchise declared' beat — historically the 2015 ISGS split.

name: Azawad National Front
composite_of: Composite closely modeled on the Tuareg national movement lineage: MNLA (2012) → CMA → FLA (2025–26). Separatist, ethno-territorial, fights and bargains by turns.
notes: No external sponsor: its strength is demographic and territorial, not imported (ExternalSupport = 0, §18.5). The April 2026 coordinated offensives with JNIM — ideologically unlike groups allying out of shared interest — are the networking surprise this faction exists to make possible (§5.2).
